title: How to Make Moroccan Preserved Lemons with Sea Salt
description: Moroccan preserved lemons are easy to make using just two ingredients: fresh lemons and sea salt. This salt-cured fermentation creates intensely flavorful lemons perfect for adding to dishes, with a tangy, umami-rich taste from the fermentation process.

ingredients.item: g fresh lemons (about 4 to 5 small lemons)

steps: Wash all of the lemons and weigh out the salt in a bowl.
Cut the ends off the lemons, then starting at one end, cut each lemon into quarters without cutting all the way through.
Over a large bowl, fill the crevices of the lemons with copious amounts of salt until all the salt is used up.
Pack the whole lemons into a clean, large jar, wedging them in as tightly as possible, and lemon juice will be released in the process.
Once the jar is full, make sure all the lemons are submerged in lemon juice, adding juice from extra lemons if needed.
Place a lid on the jar and allow the lemons to cure at room temperature for 4 weeks.
Once opened, store in the fridge indefinitely, rinsing off any excess salt and brine before using the preserved lemons in recipes, and discarding the pulp to use only the well-rinsed rind.
